summary: Build 215 is a LOCAL-ONLY packet that CLOSES OUT the one approved, Jason-operated, SUCCESSFUL guarded controlled live roofer SMS send of Build 210/211/214 selected variant R1 (new_lead_fast_response_alert) using the Build 213 fixed/guarded message binding. It records the observed terminal result of that single approved guarded attempt — names/booleans/codes ONLY — gate_decision_before_execution=CONTROLLED_LIVE_SMS_PERMITTED, message_binding bound_to_approved_selected_variant=true and outbound_body_equals_approved_selected_variant=true, preflight_status=PERMITTED, send_attempt_count=1, sms_sent=true, retry_performed=false, final_decision=CONTROLLED_LIVE_SMS_ONE_MESSAGE_SENT, exit_status=0, the single-use approval is now consumed/expired, and the post-send env-clear check passed. CRITICAL NEW PROOF: Jason confirmed the actual received text matched the approved R1 message EXACTLY (not the stale generic delivery-test copy), so the R1 message binding is now validated LIVE and R1 value-message delivery is proven end-to-end. It records the strategic next decision packet (GUARDED_R1_LIVE_DELIVERY_CONFIRMED_SMALL_ROOFER_PILOT_READY_FOR_APPROVAL, recommended_next_option=small_consenting_roofer_workflow_validation_packet, authorizes_send_now=false), a lean fastest-pilot-path to small consenting roofer workflow validation/selling, and a lean feedback-capture template. This build performs NO live action: no SMS, no retry, no Twilio, no Twilio client, no messages.create, no network/external call, no roofer/homeowner contact, no confirm-token arming, and reads/records NO secret values, SIDs, tokens, credential values, phone numbers, or production data. Capturing the closeout does NOT send. Any further live send requires a NEW, fresh, signed, per-attempt approval. Launch remains pilot-gated, NOT unrestricted. Safety posture preserved: demo_ready_with_live_automation_disabled.

guarded_r1_send_closeout_evidence.approved_message_text: RoofLeadHQ: New roofing lead just came in. A quick first reply now helps you reach them while they're still looking. Reply STOP to opt out.
guarded_r1_send_closeout_evidence.closeout_notes: This records the observed terminal result of the ONE approved, Jason-operated, GUARDED controlled live roofer R1 SMS attempt. The gate permitted execution (CONTROLLED_LIVE_SMS_PERMITTED), the message binding was bound to the approved selected variant and the outbound body equalled the approved R1 text exactly, send-time preflight was PERMITTED, exactly one send attempt was made, the SMS was sent (sms_sent=true), no retry was performed, the final decision was CONTROLLED_LIVE_SMS_ONE_MESSAGE_SENT, and exit_status was 0. Jason (the recipient/operator on his own consenting test identity) confirmed the actual received text matched the approved R1 message EXACTLY and that the stale generic delivery-test copy was NOT received this time — so the R1 message binding is now validated LIVE and R1 value-message delivery is proven end-to-end (the Build 212 content mismatch did not recur). The single-use approval is now consumed and expired; no retry is allowed or performed after success. The post-send env-clear check passed. Names/booleans/codes only — no secret values, raw SIDs, tokens, credential values, phone numbers, or production data are recorded here.

post_send_env_clear_check.note: Observed post-send env-clear status reported by name only (cleared booleans); no secret value, phone number, SID, or token value is read or recorded by capturing this closeout.

fastest_pilot_path.purpose: The leanest next-step path from the now-proven guarded R1 live SMS to small consenting roofer workflow validation and selling. Move immediately from proof SMS to a sales/pilot conversation. No infrastructure scaffolding.

feedback_capture_template.purpose: Capture-only template for the small consenting roofer pilot. Feedback is collected OUTSIDE this build by Jason. No send occurs during feedback capture. All fields start pending until real roofer feedback / Jason review is recorded.

homeowner_facing_copy_boundary.note: The one sent R1 SMS was roofer-facing only. No homeowner-facing message is approved, consented, or to be sent. Any homeowner-facing message requires separate, explicit consent capture and a separate signed approval.
